claims.name: Compressed random access
claims.meaning: Jump to an arbitrary token while the sequence remains compressed.

claims.name: Search on compressed VRAM data
claims.meaning: Count and locate patterns without expanding the searchable index.

claims.name: Fused compressed matrix compute
claims.meaning: Consume compressed weights inside the matrix operation without a full decompressed buffer.

claims.name: Compressed KV attention
claims.meaning: Hold substantially more context state in the same VRAM budget.

claims.name: Native compressed KV latency
claims.meaning: At head_dim 64 and context of at least 16K, the fair warp-cooperative sweep found a small compressed-kernel latency win. At short context or head_dim 128, dense remains faster.
claims.scope: engine-level sweep; Qn=64; head_dim=64/128; context 4K–64K; fair warp-cooperative dense baseline

claims.name: KV capacity at equal VRAM
claims.meaning: Capacity remains the decisive measured value even where the latency crossover is absent or marginal.
claims.scope: engine-level compressed KV allocation; live llama.cpp dense-cache replacement still pending

claims.name: Sparse direct access
claims.meaning: Read the few required positions instead of decompressing millions of unused values.

claims.name: Cross-request prompt sharing
claims.meaning: Share common prompt state across 256 near-identical requests.

boundary: These are repository benchmark claims, not a universal prediction. The measured latency win is marginal and limited to head_dim 64 at long context on one GPU; dense remains faster in other measured regions. Production claims require exact-workload PASS evidence and independent reproduction.
